description:
## Goal

Remove the 64-byte hard cap on per-process heap objects. Allow ordinary large objects (tuples, maps, vectors with many fields) to live on the heap, bounded only by the largest block in SIZE_TABLE.

## Background

`MAX_HEAP_OBJECT_BYTES = 64` is asserted in `Heap::alloc()` and exists defensively for a future shared zone (now the parent epic). The grow logic in `alloc()` already handles oversized objects by jumping size_class via `pick_size_class(size).max(bumped)`. The cap is a single assertion, not a structural requirement.

## Latent bug surfaced by lifting

Today's grow path picks `new_class = max(want_for_alloc, bumped)` but never verifies that the resulting block size actually fits the object. An object larger than `SIZE_TABLE[last]` (~6 MB) would silently bump_top past block_end. Currently masked by the 64-byte cap.

## Changes

### runtime/src/heap.rs
- Remove the `assert!(size <= MAX_HEAP_OBJECT_BYTES, ...)` in `Heap::alloc()`.
- Remove the `MAX_HEAP_OBJECT_BYTES` constant.
- In the grow branch, after computing `new_size`, add `assert!(size <= new_size, "heap object {} bytes exceeds largest size class {}", size, SIZE_TABLE[SIZE_TABLE.len()-1])`. Objects beyond SIZE_TABLE's last entry are unsupported; document this as the new effective cap (~6 MB).
- Remove the test `alloc_panics_over_max_object_bytes`.
- Add tests: 
  - Allocate a Struct with payload pushing total size to 200 bytes; verify alloc succeeds and grow promoted to a larger size_class.
  - Allocate a Map with 5+ entries (~96 bytes); verify alloc + GC round-trip.
  - Allocate a VecI64 with 100 elements (~824 bytes); verify alloc + GC.
  - (Optional) Allocate a struct whose size is exactly `SIZE_TABLE[last]`; verify it fits. One beyond `SIZE_TABLE[last]` triggers the new assert.

### docs/guides/memory.html
Handled by the epic-level guide rewrite; do not duplicate here.

## Non-changes

- src/ir_codegen.rs: no changes; codegen never references MAX_HEAP_OBJECT_BYTES.
- src/ir_lower.rs: no changes.
- Cheney to-space sizing already handles large objects via `pick_size_class(bytes_used or last_gc_live * 2)`.

## Verification

- All existing tests pass.
- New large-object tests pass for Struct, Map, and Vec kinds.
- The over-max assert fires for objects > SIZE_TABLE[last].
- Three-path parity: not exercised in this ticket — codegen path is unchanged.
title: fz-shr.1: Lift MAX_HEAP_OBJECT_BYTES cap
